## Character

First end-to-end execution of the gauntlet suite via rbw-tP against actual fresh state. The bug class this heat exists to catch — silent first-build assumptions — surfaces here in its remaining form: case-level probes have caught what they can during walkthrough and fixture construction; this pace exposes the cross-section bugs only the full assembled sequence reveals.

**Wrap criterion is green tally.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Not "construction complete." This pace exists as a structural guardrail against the recurring agent failure mode where wrap is driven at "I built the thing" rather than "the thing actually works against reality." If the first run surfaces issues, fixes land here (small) or spawn follow-up paces (large) — but this pace stays open until rbw-tP runs green end-to-end.

Intent-only docket. Triage decisions surface at mount time per surprise.

Post-BBABB landing: this pace may run concurrently with BBABA on a different machine when both stations carry distinct BURS_TINCTURE values. Tincture composition makes RBDC-derived names disjoint per station; the remaining collision surface is operator error (same tincture on both stations).

## Cost

~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt (one throwaway from pristine-lifecycle fixture, one canonical from canonical-establish). Re-runs after inline fixes accumulate cost; budget accordingly.

## Scope

- Marshal zero per RELEASE.md §2 (signature commit on HEAD)
- Run rbw-tP, which enforces entry contract and exec's the gauntlet suite tabtarget
- Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX
- Triage routing per surprise:
  - Tiny inline (one-line bash, message clarity) — fix and re-run within this pace
  - Larger (structural, design call) — slate as follow-up pace, surface to operator before deciding
- Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions

## Collision-aware diagnostic

Any GCP "resource already exists" or equivalent name-collision error during the run produces a diagnostic that names BURS_TINCTURE and the colliding term, with a hypothesis pointing at peer-station tincture overlap. Distinguishes tincture-composition collision from transient GCP race.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
- **Wrap criterion is green tally** — explicit guardrail, restated to defeat drift
- Re-runs within the pace are first-class; each re-run draws fresh quota
- Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists and divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any retry path must respect cult-verb posture; reset between attempts goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery
- Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from marshal-zero, not patch-and-continue

- gauntlet-suite-assembly landed (rbw-tP and the gauntlet suite tabtarget exist)

- /rbk-prep-release integration (BBAAH)

- Cleanup beyond what rbw-tP itself does — operator peeks post-success and runs cleanup tabtargets manually if desired

derive depot project ID, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket via RBDC.

as configured by RBRR. Update to reflect that depot identity is now

## Character

Restorative spec authoring. BBAAQ Surface 2 inlined six cult-verb operations
into RBS0 (lines 1045-1119) as 5-7 line abstract behavioral contracts and
left the original procedural sub-spec files (RBSDK, RBSRK, RBSSF, RBSSL)
un-included on disk. The orphans hold detail the new RBS0 inline content
does not: API endpoint URLs, HTTP response code expectations,
`axhopa_arg_parameter` / `axhos_step` / `axhoot_typed_output` /
`axhoc_completion` blocks, liturgical links to Google REST docs.

This pace re-authors the procedural specifications so the new cult-verb
operations sit on the same depth tier as RBSDE / RBSDU / RBSGM / RBSDV /
RBSDY (full procedural sub-specs included by RBS0), AND preserves git
ancestry of the orphans by using `git mv` rather than create-then-delete.
The orphans are still on disk un-deleted, so the rename path is open;
preserving blame/log on the procedural detail is a load-bearing recovery
of project history.

Pure .adoc — no code, no narrative tracks — but with judgment work on
each behavioral-contract adaptation.

Runs after BBAAY (regime-spec restructure) so the depot-identity rewrite
landing pattern is settled when the new procedural specs reference RBRR
fields.

## Source orphans → target operations (with ancestry strategy)

Each target file lands in two commits to keep git's rename detection
robust against wholesale content replacement:

Single commit captures all four renames. `git log --follow` will trace
each new path back through the rename to the orphan's full history.

**Stage 2 — content rewrite per-file:**
Each renamed file gets its content replaced to match the new
behavioral contract (see "Behavioral-contract deltas" below). Per-file
commits keep diffs reviewable. Content replacement is wholesale — old
knight/charter/forfeit/list semantics fully gone — but the file path

## Behavioral-contract deltas (from RBS0:1045-1119)

Each new spec must reflect these BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts, which differ
from the orphans' originals:

**invest** (was knight/charter):
- Singleton-per-role: zero instance argument from operator
- SA name derived from role + depot moniker (regime-kindled), not operator-supplied
- Producer-output discipline: writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively;
  the `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires
- Fatal if SA already exists; operator divests first to re-key

**divest** (was sa_forfeit, now per-role):
- Zero-arg by-role lookup: reads role's installed RBRA, extracts SA email,
  issues REST DELETE
- After successful cloud DELETE, removes role's production RBRA file to
  mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse

**roster** (was sa_list, now per-role):
- Read-only observation
- Emits one fact file per discovered SA via BUS0 multi-fact convention
  (`buf_write_fact_multi` against a registered extension constant); fact
  content is empty, presence-as-fact
- Stdout-substring assertions retire from consumer-facing surface

## Procedural detail to preserve (from orphans)

Each new spec carries forward the orphans' shape:

- `//axhob_operation` opener with operation quoin
- NOTE block describing the operation's role and scope
- `//axhopa_arg_parameter` / `//axhopt_typed_parameter` blocks (or omit
  parameters where the new contract is zero-arg)
- `//axhos_step` blocks with `{rbbc_*}` action verbs (`{rbbc_call}`,
  `{rbbc_require}`, `{rbbc_store}`, `{rbbc_fatal}`, `{rbbc_show}`)
- API endpoint URLs (link form: `link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/...[...]`)
- HTTP response code expectations (200 / 404 handling, etc.)
- `//axhoot_typed_output` block declaring output type
- `//axhoc_completion` closing prose

Match the depth and shape of RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc as the gold standard
for a procedural sub-spec.

Runs after BBAAY (regime-spec restructure) so the depot-identity rewrite
landing pattern is settled when the new procedural specs reference RBRR
fields.

## Source orphans → target operations

The mapping is asymmetric: knight specs map 1:1 to invest specs, but
sa_forfeit and sa_list each split per-role.

| Source orphan (template) | Target operation(s) |
|--------------------------|---------------------|
| RBSDK-director_knight.adoc | rbtgo_director_invest |
| RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc | rbtgo_retriever_invest |
| RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc | rbtgo_director_divest, rbtgo_retriever_divest |
| RBSSL-sa_list.adoc | rbtgo_director_roster, rbtgo_retriever_roster |

Six new sub-spec files total.

## Behavioral-contract deltas (from RBS0:1045-1119)

Each new spec must reflect these BBAAQ Surface 2 contracts, which differ
from the orphans' originals:

**invest** (was knight/charter):
- Singleton-per-role: zero instance argument from operator
- SA name derived from role + depot moniker (regime-kindled), not operator-supplied
- Producer-output discipline: writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively;
  the `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires
- Fatal if SA already exists; operator divests first to re-key

**divest** (was sa_forfeit, now per-role):
- Zero-arg by-role lookup: reads role's installed RBRA, extracts SA email,
  issues REST DELETE
- After successful cloud DELETE, removes role's production RBRA file to
  mirror cloud state and prevent stale-credential reuse

**roster** (was sa_list, now per-role):
- Read-only observation
- Emits one fact file per discovered SA via BUS0 multi-fact convention
  (`buf_write_fact_multi` against a registered extension constant); fact
  content is empty, presence-as-fact
- Stdout-substring assertions retire from consumer-facing surface

## Procedural detail to preserve (from orphans)

Each new spec carries forward the orphans' shape:

- `//axhob_operation` opener with operation quoin
- NOTE block describing the operation's role and scope
- `//axhopa_arg_parameter` / `//axhopt_typed_parameter` blocks (or omit
  parameters where the new contract is zero-arg)
- `//axhos_step` blocks with `{rbbc_*}` action verbs (`{rbbc_call}`,
  `{rbbc_require}`, `{rbbc_store}`, `{rbbc_fatal}`, `{rbbc_show}`)
- API endpoint URLs (link form: `link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/...[...]`)
- HTTP response code expectations (200 / 404 handling, etc.)
- `//axhoot_typed_output` block declaring output type
- `//axhoc_completion` closing prose

Match the depth and shape of RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc as the gold standard
for a procedural sub-spec.

## File naming and prefix discipline

Acronym selection follows CLAUDE.md "mint" rules (prefix discipline,
terminal exclusivity, suffix conventions). Suggested approach for the
pace executor:

- **RBSDK** (currently director_knight): retain the file path, rename to
  `RBSDK-director_invest.adoc`, fully replace content. K letter loses its
  knight semantic but the file path is already mapped in CLAUDE.md and
  reuse avoids new mint cost.
- **RBSRK** (currently retriever_knight): retain path, rename to
  `RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc`, replace content.
- **RBSSF, RBSSL**: split each into two new acronyms. Operator/executor
  picks letters; verify against terminal-exclusivity check. Candidate
  patterns: `RBSDD-director_divest`, `RBSRD-retriever_divest`,
  `RBSDR-director_roster`, `RBSRL-retriever_roster` (verify free —
  RBSRR is taken by RegimeRepo, RBSDL by depot_list, etc.). After the
  split, the original `RBSSF` and `RBSSL` files are git-rm'd since
  the SA-grouped concept is dissolved.

Alternative shape (defer to executor judgment): if the new specs feel
genuinely sibling enough to share a single file each, it's possible to
have one `RBSXX-invest.adoc` covering both director and retriever variants
with explicit per-role sections. The existing per-role file shape (RBSDK
vs. RBSRK as separate files) suggests one-file-per-role is the
established convention; default to that unless executor finds compelling
reason otherwise.

  / etc. directives, plus any cross-cutting prose that was inline (the

  inline as cross-cutting framing).
- Mapping section unchanged (the six attribute references already exist
  at RBS0:117-132).

Sub-spec file authoring lands in this pace; orphan disposal lands at end

retained); RBSSF and RBSSL are `git rm`'d (no longer represent valid
operations under the per-role split). CLAUDE.md acronym map updated to
reflect the rename / removal.

## Character

Mechanical sweep — convert any raw `base64` invocation in BUK/RBK shell to
the openssl-standardized form established by commit 385589f4. The
jurisdiction garrison ceremony reintroduced raw calls; potentially others
have leaked since.

## Scope

- Rescan `Tools/buk` and `Tools/rbk` for raw `base64` invocations — do
  not trust prior line numbers, files and call sites may have moved.
  Recipe: `grep -rn '\bbase64\b' Tools/{buk,rbk} --include='*.sh'`,
  filter out `openssl ... base64`, jq's `@base64`, and comment-only
  occurrences.
- Replace encode form: `base64 < FILE` → `openssl enc -base64 -A < FILE`
- Replace decode form: `base64 -d` → `openssl enc -base64 -d -A`
- Decode sites inside SSH heredocs run on the remote shell — same
  substitution applies; remote toolchain is presumed sufficient
  (cygwin/wsl/linux/mac all have openssl available).

## Constraints

- Acceptance for the jurisdiction sites is the existing live garrison
  ceremony — `buw-jpG{b,c,w}` step 6 `ssh ... true` already round-trips
  the planted privkey byte-exact; corrupted decode fails SSH auth.
- No new test fixture needed.
- `-A` flag suppresses line-wrapping. Use it uniformly on encode and
  decode so single-line embedding in heredocs stays valid.
- bujb_jurisdiction.sh encode site has a BCG temp-file capture wrapper
  (`> ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDOUT 2> ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDERR || buc_die`) —
  preserve it; sweep swaps tool name only.
- With `-A`, encoded output has no embedded newlines; sweep may drop
  any post-capture newline-strip (`${var//$'\n'/}`) that was defensive
  against raw `base64`'s 76-col wrapping.

## Out of scope

- `Tools/lmci/bundle.sh` — different kit, predates the sweep
- jq `@base64` filter — internal to jq, not a bash dependency

## Character

Mechanical BCG:495 fix-up across two surfaced sites where `$(jq -r ...)` direct command-substitution captures external-tool output — forbidden outside `_capture` / `_recite` functions. Pre-existing drift (predates BBAAN) surfaced during the BBAAN cult-verb-foundation pace's BCG-flag chase. Carved into a focused sibling pace to keep the BBAAN docket on cult-verb-foundation; this pace owns ONLY the BCG sweep.

## Sites

Two sites, same fix kind:

1. `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh:1247-1270` — `rbgp_governor_mantle`'s pre-cleanup of existing `governor-*` SAs uses `jq -r '.accounts[]? | select(.email | startswith("governor-")) | .email' "${...}.json" > "${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true; z_governor_emails=$(<"${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}")` then iterates with `<<<` heredoc-string. The `2>/dev/null || true` also violates BCG:520 stderr-never-suppress.

2. `Tools/rbk/rbgg_Governor.sh:341` — `zrbgg_list_bucket_objects_capture`'s pageToken extraction uses `z_page_token=$(jq -r '.nextPageToken // empty' "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}") || return 1`. Sibling `_capture` function but BCG:495 still applies to its internals.

## Fix kind

Both flip to `rbgu_json_field_capture` (BCG-sanctioned `_capture`) with appropriate jq expressions. Site 1 needs additional restructure to handle the multi-row email list — either paginated walk via `.accounts // [] | length` + per-index `.accounts[N].email` (BBAAN Step E's depot_unmake cleanup is the canonical pattern in-tree) OR jq-output-to-temp-file then builtin read. Stderr suppression at site 1 also retires (capture stderr to a discriminated tempfile per BCG:520).

## Out of scope

- Cult-verb-foundation surface (BBAAN owns).
- Other BCG drift not at these two sites (e.g. mantle's broader structural shape, scratch-file discipline elsewhere).
- New behavior; this is mechanical correctness-preserving fix.

## Verification

- bash -n clean across both files.
- regime-smoke / regime-validation no regressions vs. pre-pace baseline (same marshal-zero failures expected, nothing new).
- Pristine-tier verification of `rbgp_governor_mantle` happens later via the BB heat's `rbw-tP` qualification pipeline; this pace doesn't gate on it.

## Character

Spec-uniformity sweep across the six cult-verb procedural sub-specs landed in BBAAZ. Pure .adoc — no code, no behavior change. File-disjoint targets → parallel agents. Suitable for landing while long test runs are in flight.

## Drifts (from BBAAZ post-review notch 118c2d43)

1. **Invest step-3 shape drift** — RBSDK and RBSRK use different headings + action-verb idioms for the SA-existence preflight. Pick one shape (RBSDK's `{rbbc_require}` HTTP-404 form is a reasonable target), apply to both.

2. **Divest //axhoc_completion drift** — RBSDD and RBSRD completion blocks are non-parallel (different lengths; RBSRD has a "Proceed to invest" hint that RBSDD lacks). Pick one parallel template, apply to both.

3. **Divest step-4 RBRA-removal phrasing drift** — RBSDD describes the cloud-state-mirroring rationale in a NOTE block; RBSRD has it inline. Align placement.

4. **NOTE-block typographic style** — the six BBAAZ-authored specs use inline NOTE prose with linked terms interpolated mid-line; gold-standard RBSDE/RBSDV use line-broken NOTE prose with linked terms on their own lines. Convert the six new specs to line-broken form for typographic consistency with the rest of the procedural-spec corpus.

## References

- BBAAZ post-review notch 118c2d43 — surfaced these drifts during review of the parallel-agent work.
- RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc, RBSDV-director_vouch.adoc — line-broken NOTE-block reference shape.

## Out of scope

Code, behavioral changes, contract semantics, other procedural specs (RBSDE/RBSDU/RBSGM/RBSDV/RBSDY).

## Character

Operator-driven manual walkthrough from a marshal-zeroed state, positioned between canonical-establish (built) and canonical-onboarding-sequence (yet to be built). Two purposes under the gauntlet design:

1. Validate canonical-establish in actually-fresh marshal-zero state — its precondition probes were designed defensively, but only running against a real fresh state surfaces silent first-build assumptions in the underlying tabtargets.
2. Inform canonical-onboarding-sequence case design — walking §3 manually surfaces the case shape, probe specifications, and operator-facing diagnostic ceremony for the next fixture's cases.

Cognitive posture: real-time conversation, surprise-by-surprise. Operator drives commands; agent watches output, reads code on demand, captures dispositions.

## Wrap criterion

§2 (canonical-establish run as a fixture against marshal-zero), §3 (reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain, walked manually), and §4 (crucible suite per RELEASE.md) each completed end-to-end against a fresh marshal-zero. Every surprise captured + dispositioned (inline fix or surfaced as follow-up pace before canonical-onboarding-sequence mounts). Fixed-or-surfaced is sufficient — green tally is BBAAX's guardrail, not this pace's.

## Cost

~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt. Same magnitude as BBAAX, distinct purpose: this pace's bug surface is decomposed step-by-step (manual operator drive) and informs probe design before the next fixture exists; BBAAX validates the fully-stitched suite end-to-end.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Per BBAAN posture: invest is fatal-on-exists; divest deletes production RBRA; unmake absorbs governor cleanup. Any retry path goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery.
- Reuse existing tabtargets exclusively; canonical-establish runs as a fixture; §3 and §4 walked manually.
- No code changes to canonical-establish during this pace (it's already built). Surprises in canonical-establish surface as follow-up paces.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- gauntlet-canonical-establish landed (the §2 fixture exists for run-as-fixture step).

## Out of scope

- §1 (pristine-lifecycle exercises it; not relevant here).
- Canonical-onboarding-sequence implementation (subsequent pace, informed by this walkthrough's findings).
- Suite assembly (subsequent pace).
- Green-tally end-to-end run (BBAAX).
- Fixing every surprise inline — large or design-call surprises surface as follow-up paces.

## Scope

- Fixture-disposition concept (Independent | StateProgressing) on the fixture struct

- Suite-level fail-fast across cross-fixture boundary: verify existing behavior matches paddock contract or add policy
- Precondition-probe helper module — stub with extractable shape, populated by subsequent fixture paces
- Tag existing pristine-lifecycle fixture as StateProgressing (cases of pristine-lifecycle-gate are state-attestation; subsequent cases progress through depot + SA lifecycle — StateProgressing is the correct disposition)

- New fixtures (canonical-establish, canonical-onboarding-sequence — subsequent paces own their own probes)

Adds the fixture-disposition concept so per-fixture mode policy can be enforced at the engine layer, stubs the precondition-probe helper module that subsequent fixture paces will populate, and tags the existing pristine-lifecycle fixture with its correct disposition (StateProgressing). Mechanical implementation pace; no new test code added, only engine concepts plus a one-line disposition tag on the existing fixture.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time.

## Scope

- Fixture-disposition concept (Independent | StateProgressing) on the fixture struct
- Per-fixture mode policy: state-progressing fixtures refuse keep-going mode at engine level
- Suite-level fail-fast across cross-fixture boundary: verify existing behavior matches paddock contract or add policy
- Precondition-probe helper module — stub with extractable shape, populated by subsequent fixture paces
- Tag existing pristine-lifecycle fixture as StateProgressing (cases of pristine-lifecycle-gate are state-attestation; subsequent cases progress through depot + SA lifecycle — StateProgressing is the correct disposition)

## Out of scope

- New fixtures (canonical-establish, canonical-onboarding-sequence — subsequent paces own their own probes)
- Gauntlet suite definition (gauntlet-suite-assembly)
- Per-case probe implementations (each fixture pace populates its own under the helper-module shape)
- Keep-going tabtarget variants (deferred — no concrete development workflow demands it yet)

## Character

State-progressing fixture for §3 of the gauntlet. Cases walk the operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks for vessel construction (e.g., rbw-Odf/Oda/Odb/Odg per vessel mode) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly. Establishes handbook-regression coverage as the gauntlet's secondary purpose: ensures handbook prose stays in sync with working code so future operators following the handbooks aren't surprised by drift.

**Build-only.** Cases stop when the vessel hallmark lands in GAR. No charge, no test. The build/run split keeps the run-side fixtures (per-crucible) reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.

Service-tier — GCP credentials + Cloud Build capacity. Per-vessel mode coverage flows from each vessel's mode field, naturally aligning with the four director-mode handbook tracks. Drift between fixture and handbook is accepted future maintenance.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning at mount time.

## Scope

- Cover §3 via handbook walks: inscribe-reliquary, plus one ordain-* case per vessel, each walking the corresponding handbook track (rbw-Odf conjure, rbw-Oda airgap, rbw-Odb bind, rbw-Odg graft — case-to-vessel mapping verified at mount per regime declarations).
- Each case invokes the handbook's prescribed tabtargets in the prescribed order; asserts post-conditions named in the handbook; produces the expected hallmark in GAR.
- Each case carries a precondition probe per the gauntlet-canonical-establish pattern.
- Disposition: StateProgressing.
- Manifest registration.

## Wrap criterion

- Each case walks its handbook end-to-end and produces the expected hallmark.
- Case structure mirrors the handbook prose closely enough that a reader can match case lines to handbook lines.
- Per-case precondition probes verified.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- gauntlet-canonical-establish landed (precondition-probe pattern set; depot + SAs in place).
- BBAAc landed (manual walkthrough findings folded back).
- Onboarding director handbook tracks (rbw-Odf/Oda/Odb/Odg) stable enough in heat A6 to lean on (operator confirmed).

## Out of scope

- Engine work (gauntlet-engine-foundation).
- Canonical-establish fixture (prior pace).
- Suite assembly (BBAAg).
- Crucible runs of any kind — preserved by build/run split for run-side reusability.
- Run-side handbook coverage (rbw-Ots, rbw-Ofc rack portion). Out of scope for now.
- Live end-to-end validation (BBAAX).

## Character

Mechanical Rust pace. Replace dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` with `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` per RCG terminal exclusivity — `c` is owned by crucible). Register four calibrant fixtures with deterministic verdicts; pin behavior in `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Self-contained — bash driver pace consumes this but does not land here.

## Scope

- Delete `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` and its `lib.rs` declaration; add `rbtdrl_calibrant` module + `rbtdtl_calibrant` test module.
- Four fixtures registered in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs` (fixture-name consts), `rbtdrc_fixture_disposition`, and `rbtdrc_sections_for_fixture`:
  - `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent): 4 cases — `pass`, `fail`, `skip`, `pass_with_output` (writes `output.txt`).
  - `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent): two sections — §A `[pass, fail, not_reached]` (intra-section), §B `[not_reached]` (inter-section).
  - `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing): 2 cases — case 1 probe `Ok` then `Pass`; case 2 probe hardcoded `Err` (case body unreachable).
  - `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent): single case writes `ran.sentinel` into its case `dir`.
- All four `rbtdrm_required_colophons` entries: `Some(&[])` (calibrant cases never shell out).
- `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs` unit tests pin per-case verdict, sentinel write/non-write, disposition tags, sections registration, manifest entries.

## Locked decisions

- **Case-written file**: `output.txt` — distinct from engine-auto `trace.txt`. Only `pass_with_output` writes it; engine writes `trace.txt` for every case implicitly.
- **Sentinel mechanism**: not_reached cases and `calibrant-sentinel` write `ran.sentinel` into their case temp dir (`dir.join("ran.sentinel")`). Bash side (Pace 2) asserts presence/absence by globbing under the `BURV_TEMP_ROOT_DIR` it set for the rbtd invocation. No project-relative paths — hygiene matters.
- **Probe-Err mechanism**: `calibrant-progressing` case 2's `check` fn is hardcoded `Err`. No env-var or file coupling. Single fixture run exercises both `Ok` and `Err` probe paths deterministically.
- **Suite is bash composition**: no `RBTDRM_SUITE_CALIBRANT` constant. Suite-level fail-fast comes from `rbte_engine.sh` `set -euo pipefail` per existing pattern. Suite tabtarget (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.calibrant.sh`) defers to Pace 2 alongside its testbench consumer.
- **No per-fixture tabtargets**: calibrant fixtures are framework-test plumbing, not operator workflows. The bash testbench (Pace 2) invokes the `rbtd` binary directly — tabtargets are thin shells not part of the contract under test. Deviates from BBAAe's per-fixture pattern; justified because pristine/canonical are operator-facing, calibrant isn't.
- **`rbtdrd_dummy.rs` full delete**: no consumers reference it; calibrant supersedes the placeholder. Git history preserves the file.

## Out of scope

- Bash testbench, case files, zipper entry, suite tabtarget, runbook entry — all Pace 2 (₢BBAAi).
- New engine concepts beyond what BBAAd landed.
- Cross-kit composition.

## Depends on

BBAAd — `rbtdre_Disposition`, `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast`, `rbtdrb_Probe` already in place.

## Character

Surfaced by ₢BBAAc: depot enumeration via Cloud Billing v1 + Mason-SA gate is
blind to DELETE_REQUESTED projects. Billing unlinked at unmake time before
soft-delete; Mason SA list returns 404 on pending-delete. Operator has no
visibility into ghost depots; canonical-establish moniker selector collides
with reserved project IDs on retry-after-failure.

## Wrap criterion

`rbw-dl` shows DELETE_REQUESTED alongside ACTIVE. `rbtdrk_pick_next_moniker`
refreshes fact files load-bearingly before scan, no reliance on operator-run
`rbw-dl`. RBGC kindle owns the displayName anchor
(`RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX="RBGC_DEPOT_COMMON"`) across depot/Mason/Governor
create sites + search site. Mason-SA discrimination removed; moniker derived by
stripping prefix from displayName. CRM v3 `projects:search` replaces Cloud
Billing enumeration. Family stem bumped past current pending-delete
reservations. Specs RBSDE/RBSGM in sync with code.

## Constraints

- Burn-the-bridges: all current depots deleted before this lands; no
  transitional dual-search code.
- DisplayName-based discrimination; projectId-infix and label schemes out of
  scope.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- Surfaced by ₢BBAAc; operator burn-the-bridges cleanup precedes landing.

## Out of scope

- Wider billing-list enumeration audit (other sites may share assumption).
- ProjectId-infix migration scheme.
- BBAAc walkthrough resumption (separate concern, blocked on this).

Billing enumeration. Family stem bumped past current pending-delete
reservations. Specs RBSDE/RBSGM in sync with code.

## Constraints

- Burn-the-bridges: all current depots deleted before this lands; no
  transitional dual-search code.
- DisplayName-based discrimination; projectId-infix and label schemes out of
  scope.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- Surfaced by ₢BBAAc; operator burn-the-bridges cleanup precedes landing.

## Character

Surface eliminator: extract shared helper, add probe-mint behavior, validate. Mechanical bash refactor with one new behavior bolted in. Helper signature and module placement are mount-time calls.

## Wrap criterion

After the new helper returns, an immediate JWT-bearer mint with the freshly-written RBRA succeeds without consumer-side retry. Validated by `rbw-aM` re-mantle against an existing depot (fast iteration), then by `canonical-establish` from fresh marshal-zero — which then unblocks BBAAc retry.

## Locked decisions

- Single pace, full extraction. No refactor-then-probe split.
- Shared helper covers Governor (Payor mantle), Director (Governor invest), Retriever (Governor invest) — all three key-creation paths consolidated.
- Total probe budget: **90 seconds**. Poll cadence and backoff schedule are mount-time decisions.
- Produce-side guard only. RBRA on disk means usable for JWT-bearer mint. No consume-side fallback at the OAuth token-fetch site.
- Timeout = hard fail (`buc_die`). Pristine-tier contract; no partial-write tolerance, no degraded-RBRA shipping.
- Existing 404-retry-on-create logic preserved (orthogonal SA-write-path concern).

## Cross-pace dependencies

Blocks BBAAc pristine-walkthrough-manual: every retry from marshal-zero hits the same JWT-bearer race the moment Governor RBRA is consumed by the next case. Surface raised after BBAAm displayName fix landed (commit `0d4a6285`).

## Out of scope

- Empirical lag distribution measurement (operator chose to skip).
- Consume-side fallback at OAuth token-fetch.
- Divest paths (no key creation, no race).

## Character

Three RBRA install paths emit operator advice: Governor (rbgp_governor_mantle) and Director/Retriever (rbgg_invest_*). Governor still writes to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR with per-mantle timestamp — out of step with the singleton-assay-slot discipline D/R honor and RBS0:806-807 documents. Independently: install advice across all three uses cp where the assay slot's volatile-singleton semantics call for mv; and D/R advice prescribes local install where the operator running invest is typically not the credential consumer (mint-on-behalf-of-identity flow).

Surfaced live by `rbw-aM` transcript review post-BBAAn wrap.

## Wrap criterion

- Governor mantle writes to RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE (single-slot, no per-mantle timestamp). No RBRA bytes appear in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR across the mantle path. BBAAn's JWT-bearer-probe call site operates on the new target.
- All three advice blocks use `mv`, not `cp`.
- Governor advice prescribes destination (RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE); the "or, for assay (test) mode" branch deleted.
- Director/Retriever advice frames as operator-discretion ("move to a safe place"), with the local role slot shown as the canonical destination only when invester == consumer.
- RBS0:806-807 updated: Governor added to the singleton-assay-writer enumeration; verb corrected (copy → move); "safe place" framing reflected.
- canonical-establish fixture's assay-probe finds the post-Governor-mantle credential at the assay slot (locality fix validated against a real consumer).

## Locked decisions

- `mv` not `cp` — assay is volatile-singleton-staging; install means relocate, not duplicate.
- Governor: prescribed destination (operator self-mints, self-uses).
- Director/Retriever: operator-discretion framing (mint-on-behalf flow — invester typically delivers, not consumes).
- RBS0:806-807 update folds into this pace.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAn (sa-key-propagation-probe) landed — JWT-bearer probe call site is in scope to re-point at the new write target.

## Out of scope

- Retroactive cleanup of credential bytes left in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR by prior runs.
- Spec doc touches beyond RBS0:806-807 (RBSGM/RBSDK/RBSRK install-ceremony detail) — separate concern.
- Documenting delivery-channel patterns (encrypted USB, secure transfer) for the "safe place" advice — runbook concern.

Governor mantle predates the credential-locality discipline applied to Director/Retriever invest. Director/Retriever already write directly to RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE — operator copies stay within secrets dir per the rbgg_Governor.sh comment block ("credentials never leak into BURD_TEMP_DIR"). Surfaced live by `rbw-aM` transcript review post-BBAAn wrap.

## Wrap criterion

Governor RBRA bytes never appear outside RBRR_SECRETS_DIR across the full mantle path. `rbw-aM` "Install the RBRA file" advice copies from secrets/, not output-buk/. The "or, for assay (test) mode" branch disappears once the file already IS in assay.

## Locked decisions

- Match Director/Retriever shape: write to RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE (single-slot, no per-mantle timestamp on the canonical drop point); operator copies to RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE.
- Code-only pace.

## Out of scope

- Retroactive cleanup of credential bytes left in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR by prior runs.
- Spec doc updates documenting the install ceremony — separate concern.

## Character

Tight, mechanical change in rbgo_OAuth.sh: shift the propagation-race tolerance from SA-creation-time probe to consumer-side token-mint retry. Single mechanism, uniform treatment for every RBRA consumer.

## Wrap criterion

- `rbgo_get_token_capture` retries on `invalid_grant` + `"Invalid JWT Signature"` only; other errors fail-fast as today.
- `rbgo_probe_jwt_bearer_propagation` and its two call sites (zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key, rbgp_governor_mantle) deleted.
- The three `RBGC_SA_KEY_PROBE_*` constants either deleted or repurposed for the consumer-retry budget. ZRBGO_PROBE_STDERR_FILE deleted or repurposed.
- 30s upper-bound budget preserved (was probe budget; becomes consumer-retry budget).
- bash -n clean, theurge build clean, theurge unit tests pass.
- Live validation: canonical-establish fixture run end-to-end from fresh marshal-zero passes (depot_levy + governor_mantle + retriever_invest + director_invest), demonstrating the consumer-side retry absorbs the same race BBAAn's probe was bounding.

## Constraints

- Error-class discrimination required: parse OAuth response body via jq for `error == "invalid_grant"` AND `error_description == "Invalid JWT Signature."` Other shapes fail-fast as today. Capture body to disk so probe-style forensic file remains available on timeout.
- Progress logging on each retry attempt — operator visibility during retry window (no silent 30s pause).
- Backoff schedule preserved from BBAAn (initial 2s, 2x growth, 15s cap).

## Cross-pace dependencies

- Absorbs BBAAn's mechanism: BBAAn's probe + constants deleted; same constants may be renamed to `RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_*` family if reused.
- Unblocks canonical-onboarding-sequence (subsequent pace) which sequences SA creation + consumer mints tightly across fixture cases.
- Surfaced by BBAAc walkthrough (canonical-establish fixture's retriever_invest case failed with Invalid JWT Signature ~zero seconds after governor_mantle's probe succeeded — same race, different consumer).

## Out of scope

- Other retry-worthy OAuth error patterns (e.g., transient 5xx, network reset). Capture as itch — not today; today's scope is the propagation-race signature pattern only.
- Changes to non-token RBRA flows (RBRA file write, RBRA validation, etc.).
- Spec changes — no behavioral shift visible at procedural-spec level.

## Character

Reflective audit pace at the tail of BB. Walk the changes landed across BB's paces and determine which RBS0 (and adjacent veiled-spec) sections fell out of sync with code/behavior, then update them.

Cognitive posture: deliberate read-and-compare. Source of truth is the code/handbook surface as it stands at mount-time; RBS0 is being reconciled to it.

## Wrap criterion

RBS0 (and any directly-implicated sibling spec files under `rbk/vov_veiled/`) reflect the behavior shipped during BB. Each touched section either updated or explicitly judged still-correct. No silent drift left behind for the next heat.

## Constraints

- Read-only against code; spec edits only.
- Scope is BB's changes — not a full RBS0 audit.

## Out of scope

- Restructuring RBS0 beyond what BB's changes require.
- Spec touches outside the veiled-spec family unless directly implicated.

## Known sync points (running list — append as paces land)

- **`RBSAJ-access_jwt_probe.adoc`** (from BBAAc inline fix on the JWT-SA probe — IAM-policy-read propagation race absorption). The procedural spec describes the OLD semantic: "all iterations must pass; HTTP 403 → fatal access denied; HTTP 401 → fatal token rejected." New code semantic in `rbgv_AccessProbe.sh`: bounded propagation-absorbing retry — first 2xx wins, HTTP 403 retries within `max_attempts × delay_ms` budget, all other codes (including 401) fall to a single fail-fast catch-all with body-summary diagnostic via `zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture`. Sections needing rewrite: §"Execute Probe Iterations" (rename concept to *attempts*; rewrite "Evaluate Response" sub-section to reflect 2xx-wins / 403-retry / else-fatal); §"Display Summary" ("succeeded after «I» attempt(s)" instead of "«N»/«N» iterations passed"); the top NOTE block (intent shift from sustained-access verification to propagation-window absorption + verification); §"Validate Input Parameters" (`ITERATION_COUNT` description shifts to "max attempts — first 2xx wins"; `DELAY_MS` to "between retries"). Companion call-site change: `rbte_engine.sh::rbte_probe` defaults shifted to 60 × 3000ms (≈180s budget) for SA roles only; Payor probe defaults and semantic unchanged.

## Character

Verify the iterative-debug surface of the gauntlet suite, sequenced after BBAAg (suite + rbw-tP exist) and before BBAAX (first end-to-end run, green-tally guardrail). BBAAX commits to marshal-zero-between-attempts — without proven single-case rerun against the suite's expensive state-progressing cases (especially §3 ordain, where vessel construction is the costliest step), each debug iteration costs ~1 hour + a GCP project, and the gauntlet's value collapses to "must get it perfect in one shot." That contradicts BBAAX's first-class re-run clause.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning at mount time.

## Scope

- Verify (or add) `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.canonical-onboarding-sequence.sh` handles ordain-* cases against an already-canonical depot, no upstream re-run
- Tamper deliberately — delete a hallmark, blank a regime field, leave a partial reliquary — and verify per-case precondition probes fire with operator-actionable diagnostics naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it
- Confirm §4 crucible fixtures (tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) runnable alone against a §3-completed state, no re-ordain required for crucible debug
- Document the iterative debug workflow into the gauntlet runbook surface BBAAI owns: where to look when X fails, what tabtarget to invoke, how to interpret per-case probe output

## Wrap criterion

- Single-case rerun mechanism demonstrably works for ordain-* cases without marshal-zero, against established canonical state
- Per-case probes verified under deliberately-broken state across at least one ordain case and one crucible fixture
- §4 crucible single-fixture rerun confirmed against §3-completed depot
- Debug workflow referenced from BBAAX's docket so the green-tally pace has tooling on hand

## Cost

~30-45 min wall-clock. Reuses canonical state from a prior end-to-end run; does not require its own marshal-zero + GCP project.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAf landed (canonical-onboarding-sequence fixture + per-case probes exist)
- BBAAg landed (rbw-tP + suite resolver exist)

## Out of scope

- New mechanism design — per-case probe pattern is BBAAf's territory; this pace verifies behavior, not invents it
- BBAAX's first end-to-end green-tally run (uses the tooling proven here)
- Cross-heat debug tooling generalizations

3. Recommend a fix.
4. Await guidance.

## Character

Empirical run-until-green for `onboarding-sequence` fixture. Iterate: run, observe surprises, triage inline-or-follow-up, run again, until the fixture passes end-to-end against a canonical-established depot.

The fixture must produce every vessel downstream crucibles consume — kludged for tadmor/ccyolo, ordained for moriah/srjcl/pluml/graft. Verifying that production works against real GCP is what this pace closes.

## Wrap criterion

**Green tally on onboarding-sequence.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Same structural guardrail as BBAAX, scoped to this one fixture.

## Cost

~30-60 min wall-clock + a GCP project per attempt. Re-runs accumulate cost; budget accordingly.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAu landed (onboarding-sequence cases exist, compile, are invocable).
- canonical-establish fixture (BBAAe) for the depot+SAs precondition.

## Out of scope

- Construction work — landed in BBAAu.
- Full gauntlet end-to-end run — BBAAX.
- moriah crucible Fixture static — still phantom.

## Character

Make the existing per-crucible fixtures (tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml) gauntlet-ready under the build/run split established in BBAAf: each takes "vessel hallmark exists in GAR" as its only precondition, refuses cleanly if absent, and runs identically against vessels built by any source — gauntlet flow, manual rbw-fO, kludge.

Cases stay as direct test fixtures, NOT handbook walks. Coupling crucible cases to handbook prose breaks the reusability the split exists to preserve.

Sequence-correctness slate; not active focus during BBAAf execution. Intent-only docket. Code-level planning at mount time.

## Scope

- Each crucible fixture: precondition probe for vessel-hallmark-exists with operator-actionable diagnostic on absence.
- Reusability verified: each fixture passes against vessels built by any source, not just gauntlet-built.
- Suite-flow integration adjustments as needed.

## Wrap criterion

- Each per-crucible fixture has a vessel-exists precondition probe, verified under deliberately-broken state.
- Reusability demonstrated against at least one non-gauntlet-built vessel source.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAf landed (handbook-driven build cases produce vessels for the gauntlet flow).

## Out of scope

- Handbook walks inside crucible fixtures (architectural anti-pattern; preserves split).
- New crucible fixtures (existing tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml are sufficient).
- BBAAr's rerun proving (separate pace).
- BBAAX's first end-to-end run (uses the readiness work this pace delivers).

## Character

Convert post-charge readiness-wait from a theurge-side matcher into a
nameplate-borne fact, with the wait folded into the charge tabtarget itself.
After charge returns, the case is ready — theurge holds no readiness concern.

## Scope

- Add `RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC` to the nameplate regime
  (tadmor=0, srjcl/pluml=30 currently).
- Charge tabtarget consults the fact and waits internally before returning.
- Theurge: `rbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay` matcher and post-charge wait block
  both removed.
- Three colluding pieces collapsed: bash const
  `RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC`, Rust mirror
  `RBTDRC_SERVICE_READINESS_DELAY_SECS`, Rust matcher
  `rbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay`.

## Wrap criterion

- Charge honors per-nameplate readiness delay; theurge has no readiness code.
- tadmor/srjcl/pluml fixtures pass under fixture execution.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAs landed (fixture lifecycle is the right shape for this hand-off).

## Out of scope

- Service-readiness probe contract (poll port-open or curl health endpoint
  until truly ready). Bigger contract upgrade; blind-sleep-by-nameplate-fact
  is sufficient. Surface as itch.

## References

- Discovery clue: grep `RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL` to find the locality the new field
  needs plumbing through — per-nameplate `.rbk/*/rbrn.env`, `rbrn_regime.sh`,
  `rbrn_cli.sh`, `RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc`, and runtime consumers.
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/main.rs` — current post-charge wait block in theurge
  orchestration.
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs::rbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay()` —
  current matcher.
- `Tools/rbk/rbcc_Constants.sh` — bash const definition.

## Character

Pure construction. Build out the `onboarding-sequence` fixture with the cases needed to produce every vessel downstream crucibles consume. No empirical run-against-fresh-state here — that's BBAAs's reslated scope.

## Scope

- Rename sweep: `canonical-onboarding-sequence` → `onboarding-sequence` across fixture/section/manifest constants and tabtarget filenames; `onboarding-` prefix on all case names (existing five and new four).
- Four new cases: `enshrine-bases`, `kludge-tadmor`, `kludge-ccyolo`, `conjure-srjcl`.
- Expand `inscribe-reliquary` to yoke the stamp into all seven ordain-side vessels in one pass; remove per-ordain-case yoke prelude.
- Centralize hallmark propagation: each ordain case publishes its hallmark to a static list of consuming nameplates (sentry-tether → moriah/srjcl/pluml; airgap-bottle → moriah; plantuml-bottle → pluml; jupyter-bottle → srjcl; graft-demo → none).
- RCG named-string constants for vessel dirs, nameplates, case names, consumer arrays.
- Auto-commit pattern: kludge cases commit between sentry+bottle (clean-tree precondition); ordain cases commit at end (clean-tree for next ordain).

## Case order

inscribe-reliquary → enshrine-bases → kludge-tadmor → kludge-ccyolo → ordain-conjure → conjure-srjcl → ordain-airgap → ordain-bind → ordain-graft

## Wrap criterion

All nine cases compile; fixture invocable; single-case rerun works for each new case (mechanical confirmation, not green-against-fresh-state). Unit tests pass; no fast-suite regression.

## Out of scope

- Empirical run-until-green against fresh canonical state (BBAAs).
- moriah crucible Fixture static (still phantom; separate concern).
- RBS0 spec sync (BBAAq).

## Character

Mechanical RCG sweep. Replace four lowercase "yoke" string literals in `rbtdro_onboarding.rs` with a named-string constant so the operation name lives in one place.

## Wrap criterion

A single `RBTDRO_OPERATION_YOKE` (or similarly named) constant declared once, consumed by every yoke-as-string site. Build clean under `#![deny(warnings)]`; unit tests pass; fast suite no regression.

## Sites (entry points, will drift — discover at mount time)

`grep -n '"yoke\|"yoke-' Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdro_onboarding.rs` — currently four hits at error formats, label prefix, commit message. Re-grep at mount time.

## Naming

Per RCG/CLAUDE.md prefix discipline: `RBTDRO_*` for module-scoped constants. Capitalization in the constant value vs. each call site's casing is the design call to make at mount time — one constant with case adaptation at use sites, or distinct constants for distinct cases.

## Out of scope

- Other Rust files. The four-site cluster is in `rbtdro_onboarding.rs` only.
- Other operation strings ("inscribe", "enshrine", "ordain", etc.). Yoke is the canary; if the pattern lands cleanly here, the same sweep can roll forward in a separate pace.
- Comments and doc strings — those are explanatory prose, not magic strings.

Drafted from ₢A-AAS in ₣A-.

## Character
Future follow-up to the handbook-render fixture landed in ₣A6 (₢A6AAt). Design decision pace — likely requires some discussion of how to represent per-colophon parameterization in Theurge's test fixtures before any code moves.

## Docket
The handbook-render fixture in ₣A6 exercises 15 of the 16 handbook colophons. The one skipped is `rbw-HWdw` (`RBZ_HW_DOCKER_WSL_NATIVE`), which uses the `param1` channel — it requires a WSL-target argument to run, and the fixture's uniform "invoke and check exit 0" pattern does not supply one.

Decide how the handbook-render fixture should handle this (and any future param1-channel handbooks). Candidate directions:

- **Per-colophon argument lookup**: extend the Rust-side handbook colophon registry with a `default_args` slice per entry. Most entries get `&[]`; `rbw-HWdw` gets a known-safe dummy. Keeps the case loop uniform, localizes the exception.
- **Dedicated subsection in the fixture**: a second section listing only param1 handbooks with their dummy values. Preserves the "one case per colophon" symmetry without threading argument tables through the uniform case.
- **Split fixture**: `handbook-render-simple` (no args) + `handbook-render-parameterized` (args explicit). Heavier and probably not worth it for one offender.

This pace picks a direction, implements it, and extends handbook-render coverage to `rbw-HWdw`.

### Prerequisites
- ₢A6AAt (handbook-render-fixture) must be complete — this pace patches a known scope deferral from that work.

### Wrap criterion
- handbook-render fixture exercises all 16 handbook colophons (including `rbw-HWdw`)
- Pattern generalizes to any future param1 handbooks added via `rbz_zipper.sh`
- Test asserts the chosen dummy param1 value(s) produce a rendering exit (not an argument-missing exit)

### Notes
- Known exit 0 is not the only valid verdict here. The test's purpose is to catch display breakage in handbook rendering — the dummy argument must steer toward a rendered output path, not an early-exit-due-to-bad-arg path.
- `rbw-HWdw` is currently the only param1 handbook. If a second one lands before this pace runs, reassess whether an inline table is still the right shape.

## Character
Imprint-channel sibling to ₢A-AAS. Design decision pace — the core question is how `buh_tt` and the handbook-render fixture should treat imprint-channel colophons when the handbook is host-agnostic. Likely needs discussion before code lands.

## Docket
`buh_tt` delegates to `buyy_tt_yawp`, which globs `${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}/${colophon}.*` when no imprint arg is supplied and picks the first alphabetical match. For imprint-channel colophons with multiple host variants, the first match is arbitrary.

### Concrete trigger
Surfaced during ₢A6AAn. In `Tools/rbk/rbh0/rbhw0_top.sh:52`, the Windows handbook teaches Phase 3 with:
```bash
buh_tt "     Phase 2 (clone repos):              " "${JJZ_FUNDUS_PHASE2}"
```
`JJZ_FUNDUS_PHASE2` is enrolled with channel `imprint`; the tabtargets are `jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.cerebro.sh` and `jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.localhost.sh`. The handbook is host-agnostic (it's about "a Windows host" in the abstract), so the glob picks `cerebro` — arbitrary and potentially misleading for a learner setting up a different host.

The prior hardcoded string was `tt/jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.{host}.sh` — explicit about the placeholder. The `buh_tt` replacement (prescribed in ₢A6AAn's docket) loses that teaching signal in exchange for a clickable link.

### Why this isn't caught by the fixture today
`handbook-render` checks exit 0, not rendered content correctness. The Windows handbook rendering with `cerebro.sh` baked in is a green case — the fixture catches display *breakage* but not display *drift*.

### Candidate directions
- **Suffix annotation idiom**: document the standard `buh_tt ... "${FOO}" "" " (pick your host)"` pattern for imprint-channel colophons in host-agnostic handbooks. Cheapest; no primitive change. Accepts that the rendered link points at an arbitrary variant.
- **Multi-variant rendering in `buyy_tt_yawp`**: when called with no imprint on an imprint-channel colophon, render something like `tt/jjw-tfP2.ProvisionPhase2.{host}.sh` (synthetic placeholder) instead of globbing. Needs a way to detect "colophon is imprint-channel" — likely requires `buz_exec_lookup` or a sibling query on the zipper registry.
- **Require explicit imprint**: make `buh_tt` on an imprint-channel colophon without an imprint arg a hard error. Forces every caller to make a choice. Breaking change; audit scope unclear without a grep sweep.
- **Companion fixture assertion**: extend `handbook-render` (coordinating with ₢A-AAS) to snapshot rendered content and diff against expected per-colophon output. Catches drift, not just breakage. Heavier but pairs naturally with the param1 coverage work.

### Prerequisites
- ₢A6AAn complete — it produced the concrete trigger.
- Likely best sequenced after ₢A-AAS so the fixture's parameterization mechanism is known before deciding whether to leverage it for content-snapshot testing.

### Wrap criterion
- Decision recorded on imprint-channel handling in handbooks (one of the directions above, or another).
- If a primitive change: `buh_tt`/`buyy_tt_yawp` updated, call sites audited, fixture updated if behavior is observable.
- If an idiom only: documented in BCG or the handbook-building guide, and `rbhw0_top.sh:52` updated to apply it.
- Windows handbook (`rbw-hw`) renders in a way that doesn't silently commit a learner to the wrong host variant.

### Notes
- Currently `JJZ_FUNDUS_PHASE2` is the only known imprint-channel colophon used from a host-agnostic handbook. `JJZ_FUNDUS_SCENARIO` and `JJZ_FUNDUS_SINGLE` are also imprint-channel but aren't referenced from handbooks at time of slate.
- Pairs thematically with ₢A-AAS — both address the gap where channeled colophons meet uniform handbook/fixture shapes. May be worth groom-time discussion of whether to merge.

## Character

Two-step nomenclature simplification in the rbtd fixture engine.
Mechanical refactor with one careful read of test-coverage impact.

## Goals

1. Eliminate "case name string" as a parallel surface to function name.
   Today `case!()` already sets name from `stringify!($func)`, so case
   names ARE function names — but operator-facing surfaces (commit
   prose, dockets, error messages) treat the kebab form as if it were
   a key. Make canonical = function name; either drop kebab from
   user-facing output entirely, or accept kebab as an alias resolver.

2. Eliminate `rbtdre_Section` abstraction. Replace with flat
   `cases: &[Case]` directly on `rbtdre_Fixture`. Fixture-level
   fail-fast vs keep-going (already controlled by disposition) is the
   only granularity actually wanted. Multi-section fixtures
   (enrollment-validation, regime-validation) absorb their grouping
   into case-name prefixes if helpful for display.

## Wrap criterion

- All sections collapsed; `rbtdre_Section` struct removed.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` inter-section coverage retired or repurposed
  (it currently exists to test the abstraction being removed).
- All fixtures still pass under their existing tabtargets.

## Out of scope

- Renaming individual case functions for prose readability.
- Changes to fixture lifecycle (setup/teardown), dispositions,
  or run/list tabtarget surfaces.
- Anything in rbtw or upstream of the engine API.

   into case-name prefixes if helpful for display.

## Character

Validate BBAAX's repairs by re-running rbw-tP end-to-end on a branch named after this pace's coronet. Branch isolation lets parallel attempts on different machines avoid the commit-trail race on main; with BBABB landed, parallel mac+linux execution against a shared payor manor is now first-class — distinct BURS_TINCTURE values give every RBDC-derived name (depot project_id, GAR repo, GCS bucket, SA emails) per-station disjointness by construction.

## Wrap criterion

Green tally end-to-end. Same guardrail as BBAAX — partial completion is not pass.

## Cost

~90m wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt.

## Scope

- Create a branch named after this pace's coronet, off current main
- Run rbw-tP from marshal-zero on the branch
- Operator selects which machine the pace mounts on (mac or linux); pace does NOT use foray
- Triage surprises per BBAAX rules: tiny inline fix + re-run, larger → follow-up pace

## Collision-aware diagnostic

Any GCP "resource already exists" or equivalent name-collision error during the run produces a diagnostic that names BURS_TINCTURE and the colliding term, with a hypothesis pointing at peer-station tincture overlap. Distinguishes tincture-composition collision from transient GCP race.

## Locked decisions

- Branch name == pace coronet (identity-stable; no human-meaningful name)
- Branch lives until pace wraps; deletion is operator territory

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBABB landed (BURS_TINCTURE composition; the parallel-station property depends on it)

## Character

Tightens charge invariant; aligns docker compose YAML with established
quoin vocabulary; fixes pre-existing spec drift in network-name quoins.

## Wrap criterion

Charge refuses to start if any docker network on the host matches
`*{nameplate}_{enclave,transit}` regardless of compose project name.
Refusal message names each offending network plus exact cleanup
commands. RBS0 network-name quoins reflect current compose project
naming (post-ZRBOB_PROJECT fix). YAML key `default` renamed to
`transit` in rbob_compose.yml.

## Scope

- `.rbk/rbob_compose.yml`: rename `default:` to `transit:` (sentry
  service networks list + comment on line 15)
- `RBS0`: update `at_transit_network_name` and `at_enclave_network_name`
  definitions to reflect `${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}_*` naming;
  update body to use `transit` for the YAML key
- `RBSNC`, `RBSNX`: replace remaining `default` references with
  `{at_transit_network}` quoin links
- `RBSCC` step 3: widen scan — enumerate networks by labels
  `com.docker.compose.network ∈ {enclave, transit}` AND
  `com.docker.compose.project` matches `^(.*-)?{nameplate}$`,
  refuse on any match independent of current run's project name
- `rbob_bottle.sh:329`: implement broader scan; refusal diagnostic
  enumerates each offending network and the remove command(s)

## Locked constraints

- Invariant: at most one crucible per nameplate per system
- Detection keyed on nameplate, not RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX
- Behavior change applies to all charges, not just pristine tier
- No new vocabulary minted — uses existing `at_transit_network` quoin

## Open at mount time

- Whether to keep current step 2 (project-scoped compose-down) as
  fast-path before the broader scan, or drop in favor of the scan
- Defensive label match (require sentry/pentacle/bottle service
  presence) to eliminate false-positive risk on `*-{nameplate}`
  project name collisions, or accept the negligible risk

## References

- RBSCC step 3 — current behavior to widen
- RBS0:2453,2561,2567 — existing quoin definitions to align
- `.rbk/rbob_compose.yml:15,27` — YAML rename surface
- `rbob_bottle.sh:329` — current refusal site
- Surfaced by ₣BB pristine run on this host: pre-prefix-era residue
  (project=tadmor, label-only) blocked tadmor charge after MZ

## Character

Linux verification — mechanical. The fix is committed (ed898867) and empirically proven on mac (full tadmor fixture 54/54 at sentry hallmark k260508105337-691339d7); linux side just needs pull, sentry re-kludge, charge, fixture run to close the wrap gate.

## Wrap criterion

- linux: `git pull`, `tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh tadmor` (rbjs_sentry.sh is baked into the image — re-kludge required), commit nameplate, `tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.tadmor.sh`
- if fixture passes 54/54 on linux, wrap

## Mac empirical findings (carry forward for linux mounter)

1. Bug reproduces on mac Docker Desktop 28.x with byte-identical signature: sentry exits 42, `FATAL: Cannot resolve www.internic.net via 8.8.8.8` because Docker installs the default route via enclave gateway.
2. Compose `services.<svc>.networks.<net>.priority` is empirically inert on both linux Docker engine 28.x AND mac Docker Desktop 28.x — Docker picks the alphabetically-first attached network's gateway regardless of priority value.
3. Spec gap (not a regression): RBS0 + RBSSS specified IP-based interface discovery to handle the don't-trust-Docker-ordering principle but never extended that principle to default-route ownership. BBABC's `default → transit` rename surfaced the latent fragility — pre-rename the special compose path for `default` happened to land the gateway correctly by accident.
4. Fix shape A implemented in commit ed898867: `rbjs_sentry.sh` after RBJp1 uplink discovery computes gateway as first usable IP of uplink subnet via POSIX sh arithmetic (verified for /16 → 172.19.0.1, /24 → 10.242.0.1) and runs `ip route replace default via <gw> dev ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}`. Spec deltas in RBS0 (at_sentry_container narrative) and RBSSS (step 2 retitled, two new sub-bullets) extend the don't-trust-Docker discipline to default-route ownership. Compose YAML cleanup removed the inert `priority:` keys and the misleading topology header.
5. Mac verification: full tadmor fixture 54/54 at sentry hallmark `k260508105337-691339d7` — observed `default via 172.19.0.1 dev eth1` (uplink) inside the running sentry; eth0 = enclave (10.242.0.2/24), eth1 = uplink (172.19.0.2/16).

## Out of scope

- Lost-sentry-stderr observability gap — separable; capture as its own pace later.
- DNS resolve-then-freeze brittleness (no retry on transient dig failure) — separable.
- BBABC revert: not needed; BBABC's rename stays, fix lives at sentry-side ownership.

## Cross-pace relationships

- Unblocks BBAAX and BBABA implicitly: both need tadmor charge succeeding. Confirmed on mac; linux closes when fixture passes there.
- BBABC was the suspected regression source; this pace is BBABC's downstream surface. Resolution leaves BBABC in place.

## Character

Diagnose-and-fix structural — design conversation requiring judgment after empirical cross-platform characterization.

Crucible charge fails on linux because the sentry's `dig +short A <domain> @${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}` (resolve-then-freeze, `rbjs_sentry.sh` phase RBJp4) cannot reach the configured DNS server from inside its own network namespace, exit 42. Cause is a routing topology mismatch: Docker installs the multi-network sentry's default route via the *enclave* network gateway (alphabetically first attached) rather than the *transit* (uplink) gateway. Sentry's iptables OUTPUT chain ACCEPTs UDP/TCP/53 to `${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}` only when egressing on `${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}`; packets routed via the enclave interface miss that rule and hit the default-DROP policy.

Empirically confirmed on linux today by attaching a clean alpine to enclave-then-transit (compose's natural attach order) and observing `default via <enclave-gw> dev eth0`. The compose YAML's `priority: 1000` on transit / `priority: 500` on enclave does **not** influence default-route selection in this Docker engine.

Suspected regression source: BBABC's `services.networks.default → transit` rename (commit message: "eliminates compose's implicit default"). Pre-rename, Docker compose treated `default` as the gateway-bearing network specially. Post-rename, default-route assignment falls to a non-priority-aware heuristic.

## Wrap criterion

- mac behavior characterized empirically (does the same default-route bug manifest, or does mac Docker honor `priority` differently?)
- fix selected based on cross-platform evidence
- fix lands; tadmor charge succeeds end-to-end on linux
- (mac re-verification post-fix encouraged but operator-driven, not a hard wrap gate)

## Linux conclusions to carry forward

1. **Failure surface**: `rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh` → sentry container exit 42 inside ~16s. Sentry stderr (captured before any quench): `FATAL: Cannot resolve www.internic.net via 8.8.8.8`. Reproduces deterministically (identical log checksum across attempts).
2. **Why dig fails**: `dig` packet egresses on enclave interface (default route), not uplink. Sentry's OUTPUT-chain ACCEPT rule for UDP/53 to `${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}` is `-o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}` qualified, doesn't match.
3. **Why default route is wrong**: compose attaches networks alphabetically (enclave before transit); Docker installs default route via first-attached network's gateway. `priority: 1000` is ignored on this engine.
4. **The host network itself is healthy**: clean alpine on `canrbhl-tadmor_transit` resolves 8.8.8.8 fine; host iptables NAT rules are correctly installed for both `172.18.0.0/16` (transit) and the enclave subnet.

## Mac questions to answer at mount time

1. Does mac Docker honor `services.networks.<name>.priority` for default-route selection? (If yes, mac may not exhibit this bug.)
2. With the same alphabetical attach order, what default route does mac install for a multi-network container?
3. Has BBABC been exercised under crucible charge on mac since landing? If yes, evidence one way or another already exists in mac-side logs.
4. If mac is healthy and linux is broken, is the bug a genuine cross-version Docker behavior gap, or is one platform's behavior the recently-introduced regression?

## Candidate fix shapes (kept open — pick after cross-platform evidence)

- **A. Sentry-side route management**: in `rbjs_sentry.sh` after `RBJ_UPLINK_IF` discovery, explicitly `ip route replace default via <uplink-gw> dev ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}` (gateway derived from connected route on uplink). Sentry takes ownership of its own routing; survives Docker engine drift across versions and platforms.
- **B. Revert BBABC's `default → transit` rename**: smallest delta; restores compose's special handling of `default`. Undoes BBABC's deliberate "eliminate implicit default" cleanup and ripples into RBSCC / RBSSS spec quoins that BBABC also touched.
- **C. Compose-extended directives**: investigate whether any Docker-version-portable compose field forces default-route ownership onto a specific network. May not exist; even if it does, `priority` already failed under empirical test.

A and C survive future engine changes; B is fragile if Docker ever changes `default`-network handling. Choice depends on what mac evidence reveals about whether `priority` itself has fix-grade reliability.

## Out of scope

- Re-running the full gauntlet here. BBABA already exists for that and remains the structural release-qualification gate.
- Lost-sentry-stderr observability gap (worth its own pace later — fixture / charge should capture sentry container logs to `../logs-buk/` before quench so future failures aren't unobservable).
- DNS resolve-then-freeze brittleness (no retry on transient `dig` failure) — separable concern; surfaces if dig ever times out for non-routing reasons.

## Cross-pace relationships

- Blocks BBAAX and BBABA implicitly: both depend on tadmor charge succeeding. No cross-heat reference; in-heat ordering carries the dependency.
- BBABC is the suspected regression source; this pace is BBABC's first downstream surface.

## Character

Mechanical. Shape is locked; resist loop-skeleton extraction (bash higher-order is ugly — deferred).

## Source

`Memos/memo-20260513-iam-propagation-race-director-invest-gar.md` — fresh-depot `rbgg_invest_director` failed at AR `getIamPolicy` HTTP 403 because the governor's `roles/owner` grant had not yet reached the AR-resource-scope IAM cache. Existing `zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate` recognizes only class A/B (HTTP 400 + body match for "does not exist" / "is not deleted"). Class C — HTTP 403 because the *caller* is recently-empowered — has no rule today.

## Locked decisions

- **403 retry is time-bounded only — no body fingerprinting.** The bound itself is the discriminator: real propagation succeeds within budget, real denial waits the budget and fails cleanly. Same model already proven in `rbgv_jwt_sa_probe`.
- **Single IAM-grant budget profile.** Mint `RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC` / `_MAX_DELAY_SEC` / `_DEADLINE_SEC` in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (values 3 / 20 / 420 per RBSCIP). Every IAM-grant retry reads from there — no more inline literals.
- **Predicate parameterized by per-site tolerance list.** `zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate` takes (code, body-glob) pairs; empty glob = match any body. Each grant site declares its own tolerance up front. Resource-scope sites add (403, ""). Other sites retain just the (400, "*does not exist*") / (400, "*is not deleted*") pair.
- **Loop skeleton stays per-site.** Each `rbgi_*` grant function keeps its own nested GET-modify-SET loop. JWT probe (180s budget) untouched — different cost-of-waiting profile, separate constant if standardized later.

## Scope

Code:
- `rbgc_Constants.sh` — mint `RBGC_PROPAGATION_*` constants
- `rbgi_IAM.sh` — generalize predicate; thread constants through all grant fns; update each site's tolerance list (resource-scope sites add 403)
- `rbgg_Governor.sh` — apply same harness to inline GAR retry in `rbgg_invest_director`; add 403 tolerance

Specs (Tier A, load-bearing):
- `RBSCIP-IamPropagation.adoc` — document class C; replace inline 3/20/420 with constant references

## Character

Mechanical. Shape is locked; resist loop-skeleton extraction (bash higher-order is ugly — deferred).

## Source

`Memos/memo-20260513-iam-propagation-race-director-invest-gar.md` — fresh-depot `rbgg_invest_director` failed at AR `getIamPolicy` HTTP 403 because the governor's `roles/owner` grant had not yet reached the AR-resource-scope IAM cache. Existing `zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate` recognizes only class A/B (HTTP 400 + body match for "does not exist" / "is not deleted"). Class C — HTTP 403 because the *caller* is recently-empowered — has no rule today.

## Locked decisions

- **403 retry is time-bounded only — no body fingerprinting.** The bound itself is the discriminator: real propagation succeeds within budget, real denial waits the budget and fails cleanly. Same model already proven in `rbgv_jwt_sa_probe`.
- **Single IAM-grant budget profile.** Mint `RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC` / `_MAX_DELAY_SEC` / `_DEADLINE_SEC` in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (values 3 / 20 / 420 per RBSCIP). Every IAM-grant retry reads from there — no more inline literals.
- **Predicate parameterized by per-site tolerance list.** `zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate` takes (code, body-glob) pairs; empty glob = match any body. Each grant site declares its own tolerance up front. Resource-scope sites add (403, ""). Other sites retain just the (400, "*does not exist*") / (400, "*is not deleted*") pair.
- **Loop skeleton stays per-site.** Each `rbgi_*` grant function keeps its own nested GET-modify-SET loop. JWT probe (180s budget) untouched — different cost-of-waiting profile, separate constant if standardized later.

## Scope

Code:
- `rbgc_Constants.sh` — mint `RBGC_PROPAGATION_*` constants
- `rbgi_IAM.sh` — generalize predicate; thread constants through all grant fns; update each site's tolerance list (resource-scope sites add 403)
- `rbgg_Governor.sh` — apply same harness to inline GAR retry in `rbgg_invest_director`; add 403 tolerance

Specs (Tier A, load-bearing):
- `RBSCIP-IamPropagation.adoc` — document class C; replace inline 3/20/420 with constant references
- `RBSCIG-IamGrantContracts.adoc` — add 403 row to propagation-error catalog with time-bound rationale
- `RBSDK-director_invest.adoc` — mention 403 tolerance at AR step

Specs (Tier B, coherence cross-refs):
- `RBSGM-governor_mantle.adoc`, `RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc`, `RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc`

Tier C drift (RBS0 mapping, RBSHR closure, RBSAJ cross-refs) deferred to BBAAq's running-list mechanism.

## Character

Mechanical. Fold the two fast-codebase-check entry points into one tier, parallelize the slow one, expand its coverage, bring its baseline to zero, and wire it into marshal-zero as an early structural gate.

## Locked decisions

- **One fast-tier shellcheck entry.** `rbq_qualify_fast` absorbs `buq_shellcheck`; standalone `tt/buw-qsc.QualifyShellCheck.sh` retires. The primitive function stays; only the standalone tabtarget retires. `rbw-tr` drops its now-redundant shellcheck phase.
- **Parallelize via xargs -P.** Replace the single shellcheck invocation with xargs-driven parallel invocation; aggregate exit code into one verdict. Target ~1–2s wall-clock for the file set (down from ~9s).
- **Coverage expansion.** `.buk/rbbc_constants.sh` joins the shellcheck file set. Mount-time call on scope-generalization shape (multi-root parameter vs explicit outside-Tools list).
- **Baseline zero.** Every current shellcheck finding either fixed in-place or moved to `busc_shellcheckrc` with BCG-structural rationale. Fast-tier exits zero on clean tree after wrap.
- **MZ early gate.** `rblm_zero` runs the parallelized shellcheck primitive in its pre-check block (alongside tree-clean / upstream / pushed). Non-zero shellcheck fails marshal-zero before it touches anything. Gauntlet entry-state cannot accept a shellcheck-dirty codebase by construction.

## Wrap criterion

`tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` exits zero, runs in ~2s wall-clock, covers `Tools/` plus `.buk/rbbc_constants.sh`. `tt/buw-qsc.QualifyShellCheck.sh` no longer exists. `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` no longer makes a separate shellcheck call. `tt/rbw-MZ.MarshalZeroes.sh` fails fast on any shellcheck finding before mutating anything.

## Out of scope

- `tt/*.sh` shellcheck coverage (`buq_tabtargets` shape-check is the appropriate gate for generated shims)
- `.buk/launcher.*.sh` (3–4 line trivial shims; capture as itch if ever produces a finding)
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/FUTURE/` subtree — operator call at mount (exempt vs fix)

## Character

`rbfd_kludge`'s presence guard at `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1391-1448` lumps anchored-slot and origin-slot misses under one "kludge stays uncredentialed" refusal. The framing is over-broad: anchored slots point into GAR and need GCP auth (wrest stays out-of-band, correct); origin slots are public upstream pulls that need no credentials. The over-broad refusal forces operators and fixtures into an extra step that buys nothing.

Surfaced by ₢BBAAX's first gauntlet run: `onboarding-sequence/rbtdro_onboarding_kludge_tadmor` failed in pristine state because `debian:bookworm-slim` (tadmor sentry's only slot, unanchored pass-through) wasn't cached locally. In any pristine kludge flow every slot is unanchored — anchors don't land until an `ordain conjure` — so the operator-facing diagnostic prescribes `docker pull` for every slot, which kludge itself could just do.

## Scope

- `rbfd_kludge` auto-pulls origin-slot misses inline (announcing each pull at info level for teaching/visibility)
- Anchored-slot miss path unchanged: refusal + wrest remediation, preserving the credentialed-out-of-band property
- `--pull=never` backstop on the build stays as tag-drift defense
- Comment block at `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1381-1384` updated to distinguish the two slot types

## Constraints

- No new tabtargets, no handbook edits, no fixture edits
- Origin pull must announce itself — preserve the teaching moment that the current diagnostic provides
- `docker image inspect` re-check after pull stays as a sanity gate before kludge continues

## Cross-pace dependencies

- Blocks ₢BBAAX's next attempt (next `rbw-tP` run after this lands)

## Out of scope

- Anchored-slot ergonomics (separate question, not surfaced by this run)
- `--pull=never` backstop rethink
- Handbook prose update — handbook currently says nothing about pre-pulling, which becomes accurate again once this lands

## Character

Mechanical: a project-wide Dockerfile-syntax constraint, factored into its own BCG library module so kludge and conjure paths share one rule, plus the BCG cleanups in `rbfk_kludge` that BBABH's pristine-state work accumulated.

## Mint

`rbfh` — FoundryHygiene. Library module `Tools/rbk/rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh` (no CLI — utility module per BCG). Public function `rbfh_dockerfile_check <path>` — dies on violation, returns normally on pass. Predicate `zrbfk_image_present_predicate` stays in `rbfk_kludge.sh`; image-presence is not a hygiene concern.

## Hygiene rules

Applied to every line at column 0 starting with `FROM` followed by whitespace, after `#`-column-0 comments are filtered out. Violation → `buc_die` with `{path}:{lineno}: {rule} — {offending line}` precision.

1. No tab character anywhere on the FROM line
2. Second whitespace-delimited token is exactly `${RBF_IMAGE_1}`, `${RBF_IMAGE_2}`, `${RBF_IMAGE_3}`, or `scratch` (lowercase only)
3. Line does not end with `\`

Multi-stage `AS <name>` codas after token 2 are admissible — the rule constrains token 2 only. Empty Dockerfile or one with no column-0 FROM line passes silently; Docker handles its own rejection.

## Callsites

- `rbfk_kludge` in `rbfk_kludge.sh` — after slot resolution, before `docker build`. Checks `RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE`.
- `rbfd_build` in `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` — immediately after the existing `test -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"` validation, before Cloud Build stitching begins.

Out of scope: `rbfd_graft` — `RBRV_GRAFT_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE` is SLSA recipe metadata only, read by `rbfv` for display; graft itself builds from `RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE` (pre-built). Synthesized scratch+COPY Dockerfiles in `rbfd_push_build_context` and Cloud Build pod step scripts (`rbgjv*`, `rbgjb*`, `rbgja*`) are project-controlled, not user-authored.

Each consumer CLI (`rbfk_cli.sh`, `rbfd_cli.sh`) sources `rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh` and calls `zrbfh_kindle` in its furnish.

## BCG cleanups in rbfk_kludge (BBABH debt)

- Multi-var `local` declarations split one-per-line; `local -r` where assign-once
- `test ${#z_build_args[@]} -gt 0` → `(( ${#z_build_args[@]} ))`
- Extract `zrbfk_image_present_predicate` consolidating the three `docker image inspect ... 2>/dev/null` sites; stderr routes to a counter-discriminated forensic temp file under a new `ZRBFK_INSPECT_STDERR_PREFIX` kindle constant with `z_rbfk_inspect_counter` mutable kindle state.

## Vessel remediation

`rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter/` currently hardcodes `FROM python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm` — the exact bypass the hygiene check exists to catch. Declare the upstream via `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN` in the vessel's `rbrv.env` (verify field name against a working conjure vessel's rbrv.env before editing); rewrite Dockerfile head to `ARG RBF_IMAGE_1` + `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}`. Lands alongside the hygiene module so the gauntlet remains runnable end-to-end.

## Spec

New subdoc `RBSFH-dockerfile_hygiene.adoc` — canonical rule home, follow RBSDS-dns_step shape: short, MCM-annotated (`//axhob_operation` + `//axhos_step` per rule), `{quoin}` references for linked terms. Mint quoin `rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene` for cross-referencing.

- RBSAK Step 6 — replace BBABH's gap-acknowledging prose with a `{rbsfh_dockerfile_hygiene}` quoin reference.
- RBSAC ark_conjure — add reference at the build dispatch step.

Update CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings: add `RBFH` (module) and `RBSFH` (spec subdoc) entries to the RBK subdirectory section.

## Silks

Current silks `kludge-dockerfile-from-hygiene` no longer fits — work is now project-wide rbfh module with kludge as one of two callsites. Propose `foundry-dockerfile-hygiene-module` or similar via `jjx_relabel` at mount time.

## Verification

- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green
- Pristine `tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh tadmor` completes through hallmark publication, hygiene check passes in the conjure path
- `rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter` passes the check post-remediation
- `bash -n` clean on all touched modules
